sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! When I clicked on the white block in the target panel, the indicator bar moved! Before it was at [[7, 35], [7, 39]] (under the purple selector), now it's at [[7, 41], [7, 45]] which is... wait, that's further to the right. Let me check:
- White selector button: around col 36-38
- Purple selector button: around col 41-43
- Indicator was at [[7, 35], [7, 39]] which is under the white button
- Now indicator is at [[7, 41], [7, 45]] which is under the purple button
Wait, but I clicked on the WHITE block in the target panel and the indicator moved to PURPLE? That's the opposite. Let me re-check.
Actually, looking at the boundaries:
- Indicator before: [[7, 35], [7, 39]] - this spans cols 35-39
- Indicator now: [[7, 41], [7, 45]] - this spans cols 41-45
The white button is at col ~36-38 and purple at col ~41-43. So the indicator moved from under white to under purple. But I clicked the white block in the target panel!
Hmm wait, maybe I had purple selected before (the indicator was under purple), and clicking the target white block moved the indicator to... wait, the previous indicator position was [[7, 35], [7, 39]]. Let me check where the buttons are.
Actually, I need to look at the selector buttons more carefully. The white button has a boundary and the purple button has a boundary. Let me check the button positions.
Looking at the initial segmentation from earlier calls:
- The white selector button is around col 36-38
- The purple selector button is around col 41-43
- The indicator at [[7, 35], [7, 39]] would be under the white button (cols 35-39)
- The indicator at [[7, 41], [7, 45]] would be under the purple button (cols 41-45)
But wait, the framed block is purple (id=12, color=p), which means purple is currently selected. So the indicator should be under the purple button. But the previous indicator was at [[7, 35], [7, 39]] which is under the white button. That's contradictory.
Let me reconsider. Maybe the indicator position doesn't directly correspond to which button is selected. Or maybe I'm misremembering the button positions.
Actually, I think the key insight is: clicking on the target panel's white block moved the indicator. This means the target panel IS interactive! Clicking on the white block in the target changed the indicator position.
So the interaction model might be:
- Clicking selector buttons changes the active color
- Clicking target blocks also changes the active color (to match that block's color)
- SPACE performs an action with the active color
Wait, but clicking the white target block moved the indicator to purple's position. That's weird. Unless the indicator shows which color is NOT selected, or it toggles between colors.
Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe the indicator was already under purple before I clicked (since the framed block was purple), and clicking the white target block moved it to... hmm, I need to check the before state more carefully.
Let me look at the previous frame to see where the indicator was before clicking the target white block.
Actually, I realize I should just try more interactions systematically. Let me:
1. Click the purple block in the target panel and see what happens
2. Try SPACE after clicking target blocks
3. See if there's a pattern
But first, let me check: did clicking the target white block change anything besides the indicator? The main area blocks stayed the same (framed=purple, unframed=purple top + white bottom). So clicking target blocks only affects the indicator.
Let me try clicking the purple target block and see where the indicator moves.

Plan: Click the purple target block and try SPACE after clicking target blocks to understand the full interaction model.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current indicator position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] == 5:
        print(f"Current indicator: id={node['id']} color={node['color']} boundary={node['boundary']}")

# Click the purple block in the target panel (around row 10, col 7)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 7}])
print("\nAfter clicking target purple block:")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] == 5:
        print(f"  indicator: id={node['id']} color={node['color']} boundary={node['boundary']}")
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] > 20 and node['color'] in ['W', 'p', 'g']:
        print(f"  id={node['id']} color={node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} boundary={node['boundary']}")

# Now try SPACE
action(['SPACE'])
print("\nAfter SPACE:")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] > 20 and node['color'] in ['W', 'p', 'g']:
        print(f"  id={node['id']} color={node['color']} pixels={node['pixels']} boundary={node['boundary']}")
print("Done:", last_action_result.get('done'))
print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
